title: RISC-V 64-bit CPU — SystemVerilog Pipelined Design
summary: A RV64IM pipelined CPU implemented in SystemVerilog and simulated with Verilator. The design has a classic 5-stage pipeline (Fetch/Decode/Execute/Memory/Writeback), a 2-way set-associative data cache backed by a CBus-to-AXI bridge, and CSR/hazard support for exceptions, interrupts, and multi-cycle multiply/divide. Only modules containing TODO stubs (19 patched files) are represented here.
instructionPreview: This project is a RISC-V 64-bit CPU (FDCPU teaching core) implemented in SystemVerilog, simulated using Verilator. The CPU features a 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Writeback), a set-associative data cache (DCache), hazard detection with forwarding and stalling, CSR (Control and Status Register) support, and a CBus-to-AXI bus bridge.
instruction:
This project is a RISC-V 64-bit CPU (FDCPU teaching core) implemented in SystemVerilog, simulated
using Verilator. The CPU features a 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory,
Writeback), a set-associative data cache (DCache), hazard detection with forwarding
and stalling, CSR (Control and Status Register) support, and a CBus-to-AXI bus bridge.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that
all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **DCache** (`vsrc/cache/DCache.sv`): A 2-way, 8-set, 16-words-per-line set-associative
  data cache with LRU replacement, dirty-bit writeback, and uncached (MMIO) access support.
  Implement all `always_comb` blocks marked with `/* TODO */` inside the `ifndef REFERENCE_CACHE`
  section, covering state transitions, hit/miss detection, LRU replacement, offset counters,
  data RAM addressing, write-merge composition, CBus request/response drivers. (~7 logic blocks)

- **Instruction Decoder** (`vsrc/pipeline/02_decode/decoder.sv`): Decodes the full RV64IM
  instruction set including R-type, I-type, S-type, B-type, U-type, J-type formats,
  plus M-extension (multiply/divide) and system instructions (CSR, ECALL, MRET).
  Produces control signals (`control_t`) and detects invalid instructions. (~2 logic blocks)

- **Immediate Extender** (`vsrc/pipeline/02_decode/extend.sv`): Sign-extends immediates
  from all RISC-V instruction formats (I, S, B, U, J) to 64 bits, and routes CSR values
  for system instructions. (~1 logic block)

- **ALU** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/alu.sv`): Performs all RV64I/RV64IM arithmetic and
  logic operations including 64-bit and 32-bit (W-suffix) variants of add, sub, shifts,
  comparisons, bitwise ops, and CSR passthrough. (~1 logic block)

- **Multiplier** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/multiplier.sv`): Multi-cycle shift-and-add
  unsigned multiplier with signed result adjustment. Includes stall detection for
  load-use hazards, state machine control, and MULW (32-bit) support. (~6 logic blocks)

- **Divider** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/divider.sv`): Multi-cycle long-division unit
  supporting DIV/DIVU/REM/REMU and their W-variants. Handles signed/unsigned conversion,
  zero-divisor edge cases, and stall detection. (~6 logic blocks)

- **Operand Multiplexers** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/mux_pc_srca.sv`,
  `vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/mux_imm_srcb.sv`): Select ALU input sources —
  source A chooses between PC, register, or zero; source B chooses between immediate,
  register, or the constant 4 (for JAL/JALR link). (~2 logic blocks)

- **Result Selector** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/rstselecter.sv`): Multiplexes between
  ALU, multiplier, and divider results based on the decoded operation. (~1 logic block)

- **Branch/Jump Target** (`vsrc/pipeline/03_execute/pcjump.sv`): Computes the target
  PC for branches (PC + immediate) and JALR (register + immediate). (~1 logic block)

- **Memory Data Logic** (`vsrc/pipeline/04_memrw/memdata.sv`): Generates data bus
  requests for all load/store instruction variants (LD/LW/LH/LB/LBU/LHU/LWU and
  SD/SW/SH/SB), performs address alignment checks, and raises exceptions for
  misaligned accesses. (~1 logic block)

- **Load Data Extraction** (`vsrc/pipeline/04_memrw/read_data.sv`): Extracts and
  sign-extends loaded data from a 64-bit memory word based on address offset, access
  size (byte/half/word/double), and signedness. (~1 logic block)

- **Store Data Formatting** (`vsrc/pipeline/04_memrw/write_data.sv`): Positions store
  data within a 64-bit word and generates byte-lane strobe signals based on address
  offset and access size. (~1 logic block)

- **CSR Unit** (`vsrc/pipeline/csr/csr.sv`): Manages machine-mode Control and Status
  Registers (mstatus, mepc, mcause, mtvec, mie, mip, mscratch, mcycle, mtval).
  Handles exception/interrupt entry, MRET return, and CSR read/write/set/clear
  instructions (CSRRW/CSRRS/CSRRC and immediate variants). Produces the exception
  PC. (~3 logic blocks)

- **Hazard Detection** (`vsrc/pipeline/hazard/forward.sv`, `stall.sv`, `flush.sv`):
  The forwarding unit bypasses results from execute and memory stages to the decode
  stage. The stall unit detects load-use hazards, memory busy conditions, and
  multiplier/divider occupancy. The flush unit generates pipeline bubbles on
  branches, jumps, exceptions, interrupts, and CSR writes. (~3 logic blocks)

- **CBus-to-AXI Bridge** (`vsrc/util/CBusToAXI.sv`): Translates internal CBus
  requests into AXI4 read/write channels (AR, R, AW, W, B) with proper handshaking,
  burst counting, and state tracking. (~2 logic blocks)

- **CBus Arbiter** (`vsrc/util/CBusArbiter.sv`): Priority-based arbiter that
  serializes multiple CBus requestors, selecting the first valid request and routing
  responses back to the correct source. (~3 logic blocks)

All TODO stubs are marked with `/* TODO: implement ... */` comments in the respective
files. The project uses standard SystemVerilog `always_comb` and `always_ff` blocks.
Types, enums, and struct definitions are in `vsrc/include/common.sv`, `vsrc/include/pipes.sv`,
and `vsrc/include/csr_pkg.sv`.

A successful implementation passes at least 10 of the 17 DCache tests.

moduleDag.description: A RV64IM pipelined CPU implemented in SystemVerilog and simulated with Verilator. The design has a classic 5-stage pipeline (Fetch/Decode/Execute/Memory/Writeback), a 2-way set-associative data cache backed by a CBus-to-AXI bridge, and CSR/hazard support for exceptions, interrupts, and multi-cycle multiply/divide. Only modules containing TODO stubs (19 patched files) are represented here.

nodes.label: Instruction Decoder
nodes.description: Decodes raw 32-bit RV64IM instructions into a control_t struct (alufunc, op, regwrite, regusage) covering R/I/S/B/U/J formats, M-extension mul/div, and system instructions (CSR, ECALL, MRET). Also flags invalid-instruction exceptions.

nodes.label: CBus Utility Modules
nodes.description: CBusArbiter: priority arbiter that serializes multiple CBus requestors and routes responses back to the winning port. CBusToAXI: translates CBus burst requests into AXI4 AR/R/AW/W/B channel transactions with state tracking and burst counting.

nodes.label: Immediate Extender
nodes.description: Sign-extends immediates from all six RISC-V instruction formats (I/S/B/U/J/CSR) to 64 bits by inspecting the same opcode field used by the decoder.

nodes.label: ALU
nodes.description: Performs all RV64I arithmetic and logic operations (add, sub, shifts, comparisons, bitwise) including 32-bit W-suffix variants and CSR passthrough, keyed on the alufunc control signal produced by the decoder.

nodes.label: Operand Source Multiplexers
nodes.description: mux_pc_srca: selects ALU input A from {register, PC, zero} based on decoded op. mux_imm_srcb: selects ALU input B from {immediate, register, constant 4} based on decoded op. Both check the full set of op codes produced by the decoder.

nodes.label: Multiplier and Divider
nodes.description: Multi-cycle shift-and-add multiplier and long-division unit for RV64IM MUL/MULW and DIV/DIVU/REM/REMU and W-variants. Each includes a load-use stall detector that inspects the decoded op of the instruction currently in the execute stage.

nodes.label: Data Cache (DCache)
nodes.description: 2-way, 8-set, 16-words-per-line set-associative cache with LRU replacement, dirty-bit writeback, and uncached (MMIO) direct-fetch support. Issues CBus burst requests routed through the CBus arbiter and AXI bridge.

nodes.label: Branch/Jump Target
nodes.description: Computes the next PC for branch instructions (PC + sign-extended immediate) and JALR (register + immediate). Uses both the decoded op type and the extended immediate from the decode stage.

nodes.label: Execute Result Selector
nodes.description: Multiplexes among the ALU result, multiplier result, and divider result to produce the final execute-stage output, based on whether the decoded op is a MUL/MULW or a DIV/REM variant.

nodes.label: Memory Stage Data Logic
nodes.description: memdata: generates DBus requests (valid, size, address from execute result) for all load/store variants and raises misaligned-address exceptions. read_data: extracts and sign/zero-extends loaded sub-words from the 64-bit memory bus. write_data: positions store data and generates byte-lane strobes.

nodes.label: CSR Unit
nodes.description: Manages machine-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mcause, mtvec, mie, mip, mscratch, mcycle, mtval). Handles exception/interrupt entry, MRET return, and CSRRW/CSRRS/CSRRC/immediate variants. Produces the exception redirect PC.

nodes.label: Hazard Detection Unit
nodes.description: forward: bypasses execute and memory-stage results to the decode stage to resolve RAW hazards. stall: detects load-use hazards, memory-busy stalls, and multiplier/divider occupancy. flush: generates pipeline bubbles on branches, jumps, exceptions, interrupts, and CSR writes.

edges.label: extend inspects the same opcode field as the decoder to select I/S/B/U/J/CSR immediate format
ALU is keyed on ctl.alufunc which is assigned for every instruction by the decoder
both operand muxes branch on ctl.op values enumerated by the decoder
load-use stall detection inside mul/div checks ctl.op of the instruction in the execute stage
pcjump adds the sign-extended immediate (from extend) to PC or register
pcjump switches on decoded op (BEQ/BNE/…/JAL/JALR) to compute the correct target
rstselecter selects c_alu as the default output for all non-mul/div ops
rstselecter selects c_mul or c_div when ctl.op is a MUL/MULW or DIV/REM variant
memdata uses the execute-stage result (dreq.addr = dataE.rst) as the memory address
memdata switches on decoded load/store op types to set size, valid, is_write, and alignment exceptions
CSR handles LOAD_ADDR and STORE_ADDR exceptions that are raised by memdata
CSR processes CSR instructions (CSRRW/…), ECALL, and MRET identified by the decoder
flush checks can_jump (branch taken) which derives from the pcjump output
stall uses the done signal from the multiplier/divider to hold the pipeline during multi-cycle ops
stall uses dbus_not_busy from the memory stage to stall on cache misses
flush needs has_ex and has_int from the CSR unit to generate FLUSHW bubbles on exceptions
DCache issues CBus requests that are serialized by CBusArbiter and bridged to AXI by CBusToAXI
